brief: Remote Launch Day — 8-section lead-gen event page (generative test). Copy verbatim from copy-brief.md; every section is fresh layout DNA on the extracted Remote design system.

sections.note: EVENT POSTER hero: the brand's sanctioned inset noise-panel device recomposed as a centered solo poster (no content media) — eyebrow, display heading, lede, event meta caption, two pills. New DNA vs the homepage's split panel.

copy.heading: Meet the agents that run on Remote

copy.text: On September 17 we're launching AI agents for payroll, compliance, and contracts — built on the employment infrastructure we own in 170+ countries. Watch them work on live data, ask the team anything, and be first in line for early access.
Thursday, September 17, 2026 · 16:00 UTC · Virtual, free to attend

slots.name: background
slots.role: inset rounded panel w/ noise-gradient art

treatments.note: the brand's rounded inset panel on its own noise-gradient art; ink text on it

sections.note: registration proof strip: the captured monochrome logo marquee plus a NEW quiet stat line beneath it.

copy.text: 12,000+ seats saved so far — by teams that run payroll in one country, and teams that run it in 60.

sections.note: BENTO GRID (new scaffold): 12-track mosaic — anchor 8x2 with the captured agents-card art in a media well, three 4-span mediums, MCP cell inverted to the sanctioned deep-accent emphasis card, API 4-span, security as a full-width slim bar.

copy.heading: One system of record. Now with agents.

copy.text: Everything Remote already runs for you — payroll, contracts, compliance — gets an agent working on it. Here's what goes live on September 17.

copy.heading: Deploy AI agents on real employment data
copy.text: A team of agents for the work Remote already runs: payroll, compliance, and contracts. They work on live employment data inside the system of record — and every action waits for your approval.
copy.alt: Remote Agents working on live employment data in the Remote product UI

copy.heading: Catch payroll errors before payday, not after
copy.text: The Payroll Agent reviews every run against contracts, local tax rules, and the last cycle, then flags what's off while you can still fix it.

copy.heading: Track employment law across 170+ countries
copy.text: When the law changes anywhere you hire, the Compliance Agent tells you what changed, who on your team is affected, and what to do next.

copy.heading: Send localized contracts in minutes, not weeks
copy.text: Drafts employment agreements from your terms, localized against the playbooks our own legal teams maintain in every market. Nothing goes out without your review.

copy.heading: Bring your own agents
copy.text: Remote MCP connects Claude, ChatGPT, or your own agents to live employment data — no API keys, no exports, no custom integrations needed.

copy.heading: Build on top of ours
copy.text: REST API, webhooks, and CLI. Embed agents and employment workflows into your own product, or extend Remote however your business needs.

copy.heading: Every action logged, every decision yours
copy.text: Agents run on infrastructure that is SOC 2 Type 2 compliant and ISO 27001 certified. Every step is audit-ready; nothing runs unattended.

sections.note: agenda as the brand's hairline disclosure grammar: keynote row open at rest on the sanctioned deep-accent inversion (one-at-a-time), warm-wash idle hover, typographic closing action.

copy.heading: Two hours, four sessions, all of it live

copy.text: No mockups, no staged environments — every demo runs on the same infrastructure your team would use. Can't make it live? Register anyway: recordings reach every registrant the same day.

copy.question: 16:00 UTC — The launch keynote
copy.answer: The agent family, revealed: what's live today, what's in beta, and where the infrastructure goes next. — Ana Ferreira, VP of Product

copy.question: 16:40 UTC — Payroll and compliance, run by agents
copy.answer: Watch the Payroll Agent review a live multi-country run and the Compliance Agent handle a real law change. — Priya Nair, Director of Product, Payroll

copy.question: 17:10 UTC — Build on Remote MCP
copy.answer: Connect an agent to live employment data in under ten minutes. Bring your laptop — this one's hands-on. — Sam Okafor, Staff Engineer, Platform

copy.question: 17:40 UTC — Ask us anything
copy.answer: Product, engineering, and legal on one stage. Bring the hard questions. — Hosted by the Remote AI team

sections.note: single featured quote card: the bento scaffold's one centered 8-span lead cell (start 3) — oversized statement register, name/role person row. No captured avatar exists for this person, so none is rendered (AS-34).

copy.text: Sixty teams have been running the agents since April. Here's how it's going.

copy.quote: "The Payroll Agent flagged three things in our first supervised run — one was a contractor misclassification in Germany that would have cost us a five-figure correction. It reads payroll closer than we do."
copy.name: Elena Marchetti

sections.note: PRICING TIERS (new scaffold): three white tier panels on the gray canvas; Builder emphasized via the sanctioned navy inset head band (surface/inverse — a nested inverse panel, the workflow-card grammar), never a dark full band.

copy.heading: The keynote is free. The labs go deeper.

copy.text: Every pass includes the live broadcast and same-day recordings. Paid passes add the hands-on lab, the private Q&A, and a head start on early access.

sections.note: objection-clearing FAQ directly above the form: platform-exclusive disclosure, first item open at rest, warm-wash idle hover (no accent inversion — that device is spent on the agenda).

copy.heading: What to know before you register

copy.question: Is the launch really free?
copy.answer: Yes. The Virtual pass is free and includes the live broadcast, all four sessions, and same-day recordings. Paid passes exist for people who want the hands-on lab.

copy.question: Do I need to be a Remote customer?
copy.answer: No. The keynote and demos assume nothing. The MCP lab is easier with a Remote account — and we'll set up a sandbox for everyone who doesn't have one.

copy.question: I can't make the time. Should I still register?
copy.answer: Yes — recordings go out to every registrant the same day, and registering puts you on the early access list for Remote Agents.

copy.question: How technical is it?
copy.answer: The keynote and the payroll deep dive are for operators — people and finance leaders. The MCP lab is for engineers. The Q&A is for everyone.

copy.question: What happens to my data in the demos?
copy.answer: Demos run on demonstration entities we own — not customer data. Everything the agents touch runs on infrastructure that's SOC 2 Type 2 compliant and ISO 27001 certified.

copy.question: Can I get a refund on a paid pass?
copy.answer: Yes, in full, until September 10. After that we'll transfer it to a colleague instead — just reply to your confirmation email.

sections.note: SIGNUP FORM (new scaffold) on the brand's closing noise-gradient band (seeded facts: art-surface, 54.375rem stack measure, 5rem band padding): white form panel riding the art, boxed inputs, split half/full grid, filled-pill submit.

copy.heading: Ready to see the agents work?

copy.text: One registration gets you the live broadcast, the recordings, and a place in line for early access. It takes about a minute.

copy.consent: By registering, you agree to Remote's Terms and Privacy Policy. We'll email you about Launch Day and Remote Agents early access — unsubscribe anytime.
copy.success: You're in. Check your inbox for the calendar invite and your pass.
